questions.question: Define a 'line of symmetry'.
questions.answer: A line that cuts a figure into two parts that exactly overlap when folded along that line.
questions.solution: It is also known as the axis of symmetry. The two resulting parts are called mirror halves.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a rectangle have?
questions.solution: A rectangle has one vertical line of symmetry and one horizontal line of symmetry. Its diagonals are not lines of symmetry unless it is a square.

questions.question: Is the number of lines of symmetry in a circle finite or infinite?
questions.solution: Every diameter of a circle acts as a line of symmetry, and since a circle has infinite diameters, it has infinite lines of symmetry.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does an equilateral triangle have?
questions.solution: In an equilateral triangle, each altitude (the line from a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side) is a line of symmetry.

questions.question: Identify the number of lines of symmetry in an isosceles triangle.
questions.solution: An isosceles triangle has exactly one line of symmetry, which passes through the vertex between the equal sides and bisects the base.

questions.question: A square sheet is folded once vertically. A hole is punched at the center. How many holes will be visible when the paper is unfolded?
questions.solution: Since there is one fold, the hole is reflected once across that fold, resulting in two holes in the unfolded paper.

questions.question: If you fold a paper twice (once horizontally and once vertically) and punch one hole, how many holes appear upon unfolding?
questions.solution: Two folds create four layers. One punch through four layers results in four holes when the paper is opened.

questions.question: What is the smallest angle of rotational symmetry for a square?
questions.solution: $360^\circ \div 4 = 90^\circ$. A square looks the same after every quarter turn.

questions.question: What is the smallest angle of rotational symmetry for an equilateral triangle?
questions.solution: $360^\circ \div 3 = 120^\circ$. It looks identical after a $120^\circ$ rotation about its centre.

questions.question: A figure has rotational symmetry with a smallest angle of $72^\circ$. How many angles of symmetry does it have in a full turn?
questions.solution: Number of angles $= 360^\circ \div 72^\circ = 5$. The angles are $72^\circ, 144^\circ, 216^\circ, 288^\circ, 360^\circ$.

questions.question: A regular polygon has 8 sides. What is its order of rotational symmetry?
questions.solution: For any regular polygon with $n$ sides, the order of rotational symmetry is $n$.

questions.question: Find the smallest angle of rotational symmetry for a regular octagon.
questions.solution: Smallest angle $= 360^\circ \div 8 = 45^\circ$.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a regular hexagon have?
questions.solution: A regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry (3 passing through opposite vertices and 3 through the midpoints of opposite sides).

questions.question: True or False: A figure can have rotational symmetry but no line symmetry.
questions.solution: Example: A pinwheel or a parallelogram (if not a rectangle or rhombus) has rotational symmetry but no lines of symmetry.

questions.question: If a figure is rotated by $180^\circ$ and looks identical, but it does not look identical when rotated by $90^\circ$, what is its order of rotational symmetry?
questions.solution: The angles are $180^\circ$ and $360^\circ$. There are 2 such positions in a full turn, so the order is 2.

questions.question: Name the capital letters of the English alphabet that have TWO lines of symmetry.
questions.solution: Each of these letters can be folded both vertically and horizontally to overlap exactly.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a scalene triangle have?
questions.solution: A scalene triangle has no equal sides and no equal angles, so it is asymmetrical.

questions.question: A regular polygon has a smallest rotation angle of $40^\circ$. How many lines of symmetry does it have?
questions.solution: Number of sides $n = 360^\circ \div 40^\circ = 9$. For a regular polygon, lines of symmetry = number of sides = 9.

questions.question: If a figure has 12 angles of symmetry, what is its smallest angle of rotation?
questions.solution: Smallest angle $= 360^\circ \div 12 = 30^\circ$.

questions.question: Is it possible to draw a triangle with exactly two lines of symmetry?
questions.solution: If a triangle has two lines of symmetry, it must automatically have the third line, making it an equilateral triangle with 3 lines of symmetry.

questions.question: What are the reflection and rotational symmetry properties of the Ashoka Chakra?
questions.answer: 24 lines of symmetry and 24 angles of rotational symmetry.
questions.solution: Since it has 24 spokes equally spaced, it behaves like a 24-sided regular structure.

questions.question: Point A is 5 cm to the left of a vertical line of symmetry. Where is its mirror image, Point B?
questions.answer: 5 cm to the right of the line.
questions.solution: Reflection preserves distance from the axis of symmetry but in the opposite direction.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does the word 'MOM' have if written vertically?
questions.solution: The word has one vertical line of symmetry passing through the center of the letters M, O, and M.

questions.question: Give an example of a quadrilateral that has rotational symmetry of order 2 but no line of symmetry.
questions.solution: A parallelogram (that is not a rhombus or rectangle) has no lines of symmetry but looks the same after $180^\circ$ and $360^\circ$ rotations.

questions.question: Give an example of a quadrilateral that has exactly one line of symmetry but no rotational symmetry.
questions.solution: A kite has one diagonal as its line of symmetry but no rotational symmetry.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a regular decagon have?
questions.solution: A regular decagon has 10 equal sides, so it has 10 lines of symmetry.

questions.question: Calculate the smallest angle of symmetry for a figure with 7 angles of symmetry. Express as a mixed fraction.
questions.solution: $360 \div 7 = 51$ with a remainder of 3, so $51 \frac{3}{7}^\circ$.

questions.question: Can a figure have an angle of rotation of $17^\circ$? why or why not?
questions.solution: Because 360 is not divisible by 17, so the figure would not return to its original position correctly in a full turn.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a semi-circle have?
questions.solution: It possesses exactly one line of symmetry, which is the perpendicular bisector of its diameter.

questions.question: If a figure has rotational symmetry of order $n$, how many angles of symmetry does it have?
questions.solution: The order $n$ represents the total number of positions (including $360^\circ$) that look like the original in one full turn.
